verdict_reasoning: ATP5F1B encodes the F1-beta subunit of mitochondrial ATP synthase, canonically located in the mitochondrial matrix as part of the F1 catalytic head. However, a well-documented ecto-ATP synthase phenomenon exists: the beta subunit (along with the alpha subunit) translocates to the outer leaflet of the plasma membrane in several cell types, particularly hepatocytes, endothelial cells, adipocytes, and tumor cells. This ecto-form functions as a receptor for apolipoprotein A-I, angiostatin, and other ligands, and has been targeted by clinical-stage antibodies. The surface pool is cell-type and cell-state dependent (more prominent in cancer and activated endothelium), placing it in the cell_state_induced / dual_localization contextual bucket. No canonical TM domain or GPI anchor is present; the surface tethering mechanism is not fully resolved but the pool is wash-resistant in documented assays. This is not pMHC-only.
predicted_key_uncertainty: The anchoring mechanism of ecto-ATP synthase beta on the outer leaflet is incompletely characterized; it is unclear whether a stable TM partner or a novel lipid interaction mediates wash-resistant surface retention.
